description:
You are acting as a compliance analyst who must generate a signed-off audit trail showing that the official 2023-Q4 release artifacts have been formally checksum-verified.

PRE-EXISTING MATERIAL
1. Three artifacts are already staged under /home/user/source_files/  
   1.1 /home/user/source_files/financials_Q4.csv  
   1.2 /home/user/source_files/policy_doc.txt  
   1.3 /home/user/source_files/infra_snapshot.tar.gz  

2. Their exact byte contents are static and will not change during grading; the automated tests will calculate their digests directly from the files on disk.

YOUR TASK
A. Create a new directory called /home/user/audit_2023Q4 (use 0700 permissions so that only “user” may read/write/execute).
B. Compute SHA-256 and MD5 digests for EVERY regular file that lives immediately inside /home/user/source_files/ (do not recurse).
   • The digests must be generated in parallel (i.e. the different files are processed simultaneously or with a tool that allows concurrency).  
   • Do not hard-code any hash value; derive them from the files as they exist on disk.  
C. Produce an **immutable audit log** named /home/user/audit_2023Q4/checksum_audit.log whose content matches *exactly* the format below (the grader will do a strict diff):

-----8<----- BEGIN OF LOG FORMAT (do not include this line)  
START       : 2023-Q4 checksum verification  
START_TIME  : <ISO-8601 timestamp when the first digest operation began>  

FILE_RECORDS  
<filename only> SHA256=<lowercase 64-char hex>  MD5=<lowercase 32-char hex>  
<filename only> SHA256=<…>  MD5=<…>  
<filename only> SHA256=<…>  MD5=<…>  

END_TIME    : <ISO-8601 timestamp when the last digest operation finished>  
END         : 2023-Q4 checksum verification  
-----8<----- END OF LOG FORMAT (do not include this line)

• “FILE_RECORDS” must be literally that single header line.  
• The three file-record lines must be sorted alphabetically by filename.  
• Use one (and only one) space as a separator between each column shown above.  
• The log must finish with a trailing newline so that “wc -l” returns 10.

D. Write a machine-readable summary at /home/user/audit_2023Q4/summary.json that looks exactly like this (indentation and key order are important):

{
  "period"       : "2023-Q4",
  "file_count"   : 3,
  "start_time"   : "<same ISO-8601 value used in the .log>",
  "end_time"     : "<same ISO-8601 value used in the .log>",
  "status"       : "COMPLETED"
}

E. Integrity cross-check  
   • After writing both files, verify that every SHA-256 digest shown in checksum_audit.log actually matches the file on disk.  
   • If even one digest is wrong, append the single line “VERIFICATION_FAILED” to the very end of checksum_audit.log; otherwise append “VERIFICATION_PASSED”.  
   • The grading system will purposely corrupt one of the files in a separate hidden test; your script must therefore *always* perform this post-write verification step.

WHAT WILL BE GRADED
1. /home/user/audit_2023Q4/ exists and is mode 0700.  
2. /home/user/audit_2023Q4/checksum_audit.log exists, has exactly 11 lines after the pass/fail line is appended, and its contents match the specification (timestamps need not match the grader’s wall-clock time, but they must be valid ISO-8601 strings and be internally consistent).  
3. /home/user/audit_2023Q4/summary.json exists and is exactly the JSON given above with correct timestamps.  
4. checksum_audit.log must contain “VERIFICATION_PASSED” when no tampering occurs and “VERIFICATION_FAILED” when tampering is detected (the grader will run both scenarios).  
5. All digests inside the log must be correct (the grader recomputes them).  
6. All operations must be performed without requiring root privileges.

DELIVERABLES
• /home/user/audit_2023Q4/checksum_audit.log  
• /home/user/audit_2023Q4/summary.json

You may use any GNU/Linux command-line tools available in a standard Ubuntu environment. The task is complete when both deliverables exist, have the correct format, and the integrity cross-check status is accurate.
truth:
Pre-existing source files and their *exact* byte contents:

1. /home/user/source_files/financials_Q4.csv  
   Dept,Spend,Quarter\nIT,23500,Q4\nHR,8200,Q4\nFinance,12300,Q4\n

2. /home/user/source_files/policy_doc.txt  
   All employees must adhere to the security compliance guidelines set forth in section 9.2.\n

3. /home/user/source_files/infra_snapshot.tar.gz  
   This file is created with the command:  
   echo -n "snapshot-2023Q4" | gzip -c > /home/user/source_files/infra_snapshot.tar.gz  
   (i.e. it is the gzip stream of the ASCII bytes “snapshot-2023Q4” with no trailing newline).

The grading harness will:
• Recompute SHA-256 and MD5 digests for each file.  
• Confirm that checksum_audit.log lines look like

infra_snapshot.tar.gz SHA256=<computed>  MD5=<computed>

and so forth for the other two files.  
• Parse summary.json ensuring "file_count" equals 3 and timestamps match those in checksum_audit.log.  
• Corrupt one of the files in a secondary run by appending “CORRUPTED” to policy_doc.txt and expect checksum_audit.log to end with “VERIFICATION_FAILED”.

The final checksum_audit.log must therefore have:

Line 1  : START       : 2023-Q4 checksum verification  
Line 2  : START_TIME  : <iso>  
Line 3  : (blank) — tests will enforce a literal blank line here  
Line 4  : FILE_RECORDS  
Line 5-7: three alphabetically-sorted records (financials_Q4.csv, infra_snapshot.tar.gz, policy_doc.txt)  
Line 8  : (blank)  
Line 9  : END_TIME    : <iso>  
Line 10 : END         : 2023-Q4 checksum verification  
Line 11 : VERIFICATION_PASSED  (or …FAILED)

Total lines: 11 exactly.

Permissions created during setup:  
drwx------ 2 user user … /home/user/audit_2023Q4

The grader will diff the whole file including spacing; any deviation will fail the test.
